events.title: Development finance gap risks reversing decades of progress
events.description: Global fragmentation, deepening geopolitical tensions and conflicts are putting decades of development progress at risk, the UN warned in a report published on Thursday - calling for stepping up investment to meet internationally agreed goals.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Development finance gap risks reversing decades of progress

events.title: Iran threatens to end ceasefire over Hezbollah's exclusion from truce deal
events.description: Iran says the U.S. must choose between ceasefire or war via Israel as Tuesday's ceasefire deal excludes Tehran-backed terrorist group Hezbollah.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran threatens to end ceasefire over Hezbollah's exclusion from truce deal

events.title: Miner rescued alive after spending nearly 2 weeks trapped underground in flooded area
events.description: A Mexican miner was rescued after spending 13 days trapped underground, officials said, while one miner was found dead and another remains missing.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Miner rescued alive after spending nearly 2 weeks trapped underground in flooded area

events.title: Ukraine: Attacks continue as Orthodox Easter ceasefire nears
events.description: Russia has continued to pound Ukraine in the hours leading up to a 36-hour pause in its war of aggression. Three Ukrainians were killed overnight and dozens were injured in drone attacks on Odesa, Poltava and Sumy.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: Attacks continue as Orthodox Easter ceasefire nears

events.title: New relief for households being considered as Albanese government warns of ‘long tail’ from Iran war
events.description: Catherine King says while peace talks were ‘best chance’ at lowering fuel prices, further help may be included in budget Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Track Australia’s fuel prices, service station outages and shipments in charts Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Albanese government is contemplating further relief for struggling households and businesses in next month’s federal budget, as peace talks continue between the US and Iran amid a fragile ceasefire. The infrastructure minister, Catherine King, said the success of those talks was the “best chance” at bringing down fuel prices. But she warned there would be a “long tail” from the crisis even if the strait of Hormuz – which was still being blocked by Iran and strangling global oil supplies – reopened imminently. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New relief for households being considered as Albanese government warns of ‘long tail’ from Iran war

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 9 April: UN condemns massive strikes in Lebanon, as concerns grow over ceasefire
events.description: With a US-Iran ceasefire offering a fragile glimmer of hope after weeks of conflict, violence continues to reverberate across the Middle East. Massive airstrikes in Lebanon have caused heavy civilian casualties and widespread destruction, drawing strong UN condemnation. As humanitarian needs deepen and diplomatic efforts intensify, the situation remains highly volatile. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 9 April: UN condemns massive strikes in Lebanon, as concerns grow over ceasefire

events.title: Lebanon airstrike casualties ‘still under the rubble’ as ambulances, hospitals face new threats
events.description: With Lebanon still reeling from Israel’s devastating airstrikes on 8 April, UN humanitarians reported new fears of attacks on ambulances and looming food shortages in the south of the country on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon airstrike casualties ‘still under the rubble’ as ambulances, hospitals face new threats

events.title: Over 1,000 humanitarians have been killed in three years, Security Council hears
events.description: At least 326 humanitarians were killed in the line of duty across 21 countries during 2025, bringing the total killed over three years to over 1,010. The International Red Cross warned the Security Council on Wednesday that “we are losing our humanity in war.”
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Over 1,000 humanitarians have been killed in three years, Security Council hears

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 8 April: US-Iran ceasefire announced; strikes continue in Lebanon
events.description: After nearly 40 days of intense hostilities across the Middle East – marked by rising civilian casualties and widespread damage to critical infrastructure – a two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran has been announced. While the UN Secretary-General has welcomed the move as a step toward a broader peace, reports of continued Israeli strikes and mass casualties across Lebanon underscore the fragility of the situation. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 8 April: US-Iran ceasefire announced; strikes continue in Lebanon

events.title: Wild chimpanzees recorded waging ‘civil war’ with coordinated attacks between two groups
events.description: New study describes what may be the first case of a unified community of chimps, in Uganda, turning on itself On a June day in 2015, primatologist Aaron Sandel was quietly observing a small cluster of the Ngogo chimpanzee group in Uganda’s Kibale national park when he noticed something strange. As other members of the chimpanzees’ wider group moved closer through the forest, the chimpanzees in front of him began to display nervous behaviour. They grimaced and touched each other for reassurance, acting more like they were about to meet strangers than close companions. In hindsight, Sandel said, that moment was the first sign of what would become a years-long bloody conflict between a once close-knit group of chimps. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Wild chimpanzees recorded waging ‘civil war’ with coordinated attacks between two groups

events.title: Pro-Palestinian rally in London: Over 500 arrested
events.description: Police in London arrested more than 500 pro-Palestinian protesters on Saturday during a demonstration in support of the banned group Palestine Action. The government designated the group a terrorist organization in July, but the ban is currently being challenged in court. Emily Boyle reports.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Pro-Palestinian rally in London: Over 500 arrested

events.title: Russian strikes on Odesa kill 2 ahead of Orthodox Easter ceasefire
events.description: Russian drone strikes killed at least two people and wounded two others in the Ukrainian city of Odesa overnight Saturday, hitting a residential area
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russian strikes on Odesa kill 2 ahead of Orthodox Easter ceasefire

events.title: Ireland clears refinery blockade as fuel protests trigger nationwide shortages
events.description: Authorities used force to reopen the country's only refinery, after days of disruption over petrol prices left fuel pumps dry and threatened emergency services operations.

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 10 April: Fear and uncertainty grow in Lebanon as conflict grinds on
events.description: Fighting continues across parts of the Middle East, with renewed exchanges between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon. Humanitarian needs are deepening, with rising civilian casualties, mounting displacement and growing strain on services. Diplomatic efforts continue with high stakes negotiations due to start Saturday between the US and Iran over the faltering ceasefire deal. Lebanon is an active war zone and the Strait of Hormuz is still effectively closed. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 10 April: Fear and uncertainty grow in Lebanon as conflict grinds on

events.title: Iran war lands 'triple blow' to flood-ravaged Sri Lankans
events.description: The Iran war could not have come at a worse time for Sri Lanka, reeling from devastating floods and an economic crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Iran war lands 'triple blow' to flood-ravaged Sri Lankans

events.title: Lebanon: Health system overwhelmed following a ‘horrific’ day of Israeli strikes
events.description: The scale and speed of destruction from the wave of airstrikes in Lebanon which began just hours after the US-Iran ceasefire announcement, has left the country’s already strained health system struggling to cope, according to the World Health Organization (WHO).
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon: Health system overwhelmed following a ‘horrific’ day of Israeli strikes

events.title: Human rights groups decry US plan for Guantánamo camp for Cuban migrants
events.description: Exclusive: Dozens of organizations write to Congress after general announced plan to ‘deal with’ those fleeing any humanitarian crisis on the island Dozens of US and international human rights organizations are decrying the Trump administration ’s plans to establish a migrant “camp” for fleeing Cubans at the Guantánamo Bay military base if the island nation’s crisis worsens under pressure from the US, according to a letter to members of Congress on Friday. The 85 groups plan to submit the joint letter, exclusively shared with the Guardian, to US senators and House representatives, expressing their “profound concern” with comments made last month by a top Department of Defense commander, and describing any prospect of further migrant detention at the base as “deeply troubling and unacceptable”. Continue reading...

events.title: New Zealand’s North Island braces for Cyclone Vaianu with thousands ordered to evacuate
events.description: Vaianu, forecast to bring heavy rain and winds of up to 130 kmh (80 mph), is expected to hit on Sunday Thousands of New Zealanders were ordered to evacuate their homes on Saturday as the country’s North Island braced for Cyclone Vaianu, which authorities warned could cause coastal flooding and landslides. Vaianu, forecast to bring heavy rain and winds of up to 130 km/h (80 mp/h), was expected to hit on Sunday, then pass west of the remote Chatham Islands on Monday, the country’s weather forecaster said. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ New Zealand’s North Island braces for Cyclone Vaianu with thousands ordered to evacuate

events.title: US and Iran end 21-hour ceasefire talks without agreement before Vance departs Pakistan
events.description: The United States and Iran ended a historic round of face-to-face talks early Sunday without reaching an agreement and the fate of the fragile, two-week ceasefire still unclear. Vice President JD Vance, who led the US delegation during the 21 hours of talks in Pakistan's capital Islamabad, said negotiations finished without a deal after the Iranians refused to accept American terms to refrain from developing a nuclear weapon. France24 correspondent Fraser Jackson shares his analysis from Washington.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US and Iran end 21-hour ceasefire talks without agreement before Vance departs Pakistan

events.title: Haiti’s freefall demands urgent global action as millions face hunger and violence
events.description: Haiti is facing “one of the most severe and rapidly deteriorating humanitarian crises in the Western Hemisphere,” a senior UN aid official warned on Friday, underscoring the need for continued global attention to alleviate suffering there.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Haiti’s freefall demands urgent global action as millions face hunger and violence

events.title: Iran must not charge tolls in Strait of Hormuz, UN maritime chief says

events.title: Floods, power outages and hundreds evacuated as Cyclone Vaianu lashes New Zealand’s North Island
events.description: Cyclone crossed coast near Maketu peninsula, packing destructive winds exceeding 130km/h (80 mph), heavy rain and large swells Cyclone Vaianu made landfall in New Zealand’s North Island on Sunday, triggering floods, power outages and forcing hundreds to evacuate . The cyclone crossed the coast near the Maketu peninsula, packing destructive winds exceeding 130km/h (80 mph), heavy rain and large swells, national weather provider MetService said, describing Vaianu as a “life-threatening” system. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Floods, power outages and hundreds evacuated as Cyclone Vaianu lashes New Zealand’s North Island

events.title: 'We have to get ready for large-scale conflicts... we need a different military'

events.ticker_label: 🪖 'We have to get ready for large-scale conflicts... we need a different military'

events.title: Trump ‘reaping bitter fruit’ of thinking Iran intervention as easy as Venezuela, says former diplomat
events.description: John Feeley says US president was ‘flush with victory’ of Maduro capture and could make same mistake in Cuba Donald Trump is “reaping the bitter fruit” of erroneously thinking that the capture of Venezuela’s president, Nicolás Maduro, offered a blueprint for toppling the Iranian regime, according to one of the US state department’s most respected former Latin America experts. John Feeley, a Marine helicopter pilot who later served as the US ambassador to Panama, believed Trump had been “flush with the victory from Venezuela” when he made the ill-fated decision to attack Iran in February, leaving a trail of destruction across the Middle East and dealing a hammer blow to the global economy. Continue reading...

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 7 April: Humanitarian and economic pressures grow; Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Hostilities continue across the Middle East, with ongoing strikes, rising civilian impacts and mounting pressure on critical infrastructure and humanitarian services. On the diplomatic track in New York, China and Russia vetoed a Security Council resolution put forward by Gulf States to ensure safety and security of ships in the Strait of Hormuz - find full coverage here. Meanwhile, displacement remains high, aid access is constrained. Human rights chief Volker Türk says President Trump’s threat to annihilate Iranian civilisation is “sickening”. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 7 April: Humanitarian and economic pressures grow; Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz

events.title: What's it like to cover the Ukraine war as the world's attention is focused on Iran?

events.ticker_label: 🪖 What's it like to cover the Ukraine war as the world's attention is focused on Iran?

events.title: Concerns raised about motorbike tourist trail after death of British teenager in Vietnam
events.description: Local people say road conditions are rugged and weather unpredictable, while some say it has become too congested The recent death of a British gap-year student on the Ha Giang loop , a popular motorcycle tour through the mountains in north Vietnam, has heightened concerns about a trail reputed to be one of the most dangerous in the country. Orla Wates, 19, from Surrey, was riding as a pillion passenger when she fell off and was hit by an oncoming truck , according to local media. She was taken to hospital in Hanoi, where she died from her injuries last week. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Concerns raised about motorbike tourist trail after death of British teenager in Vietnam

events.title: What could come after a rules-based world order?
events.description: The war between the US, Israel and Iran was, for many, a sign that the rules-based world order, a set of principles developed after World War II, is collapsing. If that era is over, what could be next?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What could come after a rules-based world order?

events.title: Security Council: Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The UN Security Council has failed to adopt a resolution aiming to boost security in the Strait of Hormuz as the critical shipping corridor remains largely closed to global trade and the transport of humanitarian aid, with war continuing to rage across the region.

events.title: Weather tracker: Cyclone Maila batters Solomon Islands with 115mph winds
events.description: Powerful storm brings destruction, while temperatures soar in Vietnam and torrential rain lashes South Korea Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila, currently in the Solomon Sea, is expected to continue moving south-westwards over the coming days. According to the Australian Bureau of Meteorology, Maila had peak sustained winds of 115mph (185km/h), with gusts up to 160mph on Thursday, making it the strongest cyclone recorded this far north in the Solomon Sea. The storm has caused widespread damage across the Solomon Islands, particularly in Western, Choiseul and Isabel provinces, where schools, clinics and homes have been damaged. The government is prioritising humanitarian assistance after about 120 people were displaced and almost 73,000 people affected overall. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: Cyclone Maila batters Solomon Islands with 115mph winds

events.title: Pope Leo says 'delusion of omnipotence' is fueling U.S.-Israeli war in Iran
events.description: In the first weeks of the war, the Chicago-born Leo was initially reluctant to publicly condemn the violence and limited his comments to muted appeals for peace and dialogue. But Leo stepped up his criticism starting on Palm Sunday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pope Leo says 'delusion of omnipotence' is fueling U.S.-Israeli war in Iran

events.title: Leo XIV to make history with first-ever papal visit to Algeria
events.description: No pope has ever set foot in Algeria. That changes on Monday, when Pope Leo XIV opens an African tour with a two-day visit to the Muslim-majority country. The Vatican frames it as a milestone for interfaith dialogue. Algiers, increasingly isolated on the world stage, has its own reasons to welcome the publicity.

events.title: 'The Last Cedar of Lebanon': A play exploring war’s lasting scars
events.description: Aïda Asgharzadeh was widely praised for her powerful exploration of dissidence and exile in Persian Dolls, weaving her own family’s story into a drama spanning Iran and France. Her deft use of history as a backdrop to intimate, personal narratives is once again in focus with her latest play, The Last Cedar of Lebanon, which examines how individuals respond to the pressures of war and violence – and how geopolitical upheaval can echo across generations. Speaking to FRANCE 24, she explains how creating an emotional landscape lies at the heart of her work, and why, as an Iranian, she struggles to see a hopeful outcome amid the current turmoil of war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'The Last Cedar of Lebanon': A play exploring war’s lasting scars

events.title: Iran ceasefire raises hopes for reopening key Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The announcement of a shaky two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran will, it is hoped, lead to the opening of the strategically important Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway through which one fifth of the world’s oil and gas passes.

events.title: Peruvians to go to polls hoping to break cycle of instability

About 27 million people who are eligible to vote must choose between a record 35 presidential candidates as well as contenders for the bicameral congress – all from a ballot sheet measuring nearly half a metre, the longest in the country’s history. Continue reading...

events.title: Albanese didn’t return with shiploads of diesel. That doesn’t mean his Singapore visit wasn’t a success
events.description: Having received assurances from Singapore over refined fuels, diesel supply will surely be next on the prime minister’s agenda Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese isn’t coming back from Singapore with a shipload of diesel in his checked baggage. That doesn’t mean his whistle-stop visit wasn’t a success, or that it won’t be seen in future as a pivotal moment if fuel stocks continue to be choked by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The government never expected that the quick whip to Singapore, with just one full day on the ground, would elicit a new supply of petrol or diesel. Singapore already supplies 55% of Australia’s unleaded , 22% of jet fuel and 15% of diesel. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 📈 Albanese didn’t return with shiploads of diesel. That doesn’t mean his Singapore visit wasn’t a success

The 19-year-old asylum seeker from the Democratic Republic of the Congo has been at the Dilley Immigration processing center in Texas for more than four months. Continue reading...

events.title: Susan was forced out of a disability support job after speaking out. Are NDIS whistleblower laws still too weak?
events.description: Human rights lawyers say NDIS workers and their clients remain at risk despite newly bolstered whistleblower protections Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast When Susan* came across wrongdoing at her disability support provider, she faced a choice. Say nothing, and allow her highly vulnerable clients to be put at serious risk. Continue reading...

events.title: RMIT drops misconduct case against student who accused university of being ‘complicit in Gaza genocide’
events.description: Exclusive: Move comes after Guardian Australia revealed Gemma Seymour was facing potential suspension over video criticising RMIT’s ties to weapons companies RMIT University has dropped a misconduct case against a student who accused the institution of being “complicit in genocide” in Gaza , because of its defence and aerospace research centre’s ties to weapons companies. Guardian Australia this week revealed the student, Gemma Seymour, faced potential suspension over a social media video calling for the university’s Sir Lawrence Wackett Defence and Aerospace Centre to be shut down. Continue reading...

events.title: At a concert in Budapest, anti-Orbán sentiments take center stage ahead of election
events.description: At a concert in Budapest, musicians and concertgoers express criticism of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's leadership.

events.title: In Hungary, upcoming elections could bring an end to Orban's 16-year rule
events.description: In Hungary, voters head to the polls Sunday. At stake: the future for populist Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, Europe's longest-serving leader - and an ally of Presidents Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin.

events.title: India cracks down on satirists for turning its prime minister into a punch line
events.description: India's satirists are turning Prime Minister Narendra Modi into a punch line — and the government is hitting back.

events.title: Wives and children of foreign ISIS fighters stranded in Syria with no way home
events.description: NPR visits the last detention camp for ISIS wives and children in an increasingly precarious northeastern Syria.

events.title: Peru's election: A battle for the Presidency amid political chaos and crime
events.description: With 35 candidates in the race, Peru is set to elect its 9th president in less than a decade. Amid rising corruption and crime, voters are left asking: Can this election finally break the cycle?

events.title: Germany: Bundesliga side Union Berlin appoint first female head coach
events.description: Marie-Louise Eta has become the first-ever female head coach of a senior men's side in Europe's top five leagues, following the dismissal of Steffen Baumgart. The 34-year-old already has experience in men's football.

events.title: China boosts ties with Taiwan after opposition leader visits
events.description: China said it would resume flights between Taiwan and more cities on the mainland, as well as easing bans on Taiwanese food and TV shows.

events.title: Germany news: Most Germans see decline in public manners
events.description: Most Germans think people's manners are getting worse, although most rate themselves as polite. Meawhile, there's trouble at the top of Europe's largest economy amid a dispute over fuel price relief. DW has more.

events.title: US and Iran fail to agree a deal after marathon Pakistan talks
events.description: US Vice President JD Vance says Iranian negotiators chose not to accept the US's terms, which Iran says were "unreasonable" and "excessive." DW has more.

events.title: Russian Memorial human rights NGO labeled 'extremist'
events.description: Russia's Supreme Court has labeled the human rights organization Memorial as "extremist." Experts say this represents a new level of repression in Russia.

events.title: Germans see little progress on cutting red tape
events.description: Most people and businesses in Germany say they've seen little change in bureaucracy despite Chancellor Friedrich Merz's government promises to reduce it.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Germans see little progress on cutting red tape

events.title: UK: Sudanese man held over migrant drownings in Channel
events.description: UK authorities have detained a 27-year-old man in the aftermath of migrant drownings in the English Channel. Four people died on Thursday attempting to reach the UK from France.
events.ticker_label: 💥 UK: Sudanese man held over migrant drownings in Channel

events.title: Week in pictures: the Artemis II lunar mission, a key Hungarian election and Trump's coveted arch
events.description: Artemis II's astronauts closed out humanity's first lunar voyage in more than half a century, the run-up to Hungary's crucial election and artist drawings of US President Donald Trump's planned "triumphal" arch, which resembles Paris's Arc de Triomphe. Here's a look back at some of the week's most striking images.

events.title: Italy's Crippa wins Paris marathon as Ethiopia's Demise sets new women's record
events.description: Italy's Yemaneberhan Crippa won the Paris Marathon on Sunday with a personal best performance in a tightly contested men’s race, while Ethiopia's Shure Demise set a new course record in the women’s event with a commanding victory.

events.title: Hungary: Tracking the campaign
events.description: Vendeline Von Bredow is the Senior Germany correspondent for The Economist, who's also been covering the Hungarian election.

events.title: Talks break down as both sides play hardball - what happens next?
events.description: They talked for 21 hours. The highest-level meeting between the US and Iran since its revolutionary government came to power in the '70s.

events.title: Hungary goes to the polls as its hardman leader faces end of his 16-year reign
events.description: Hungary is voting to elect its leader and all 199 members of its parliament.

events.title: Man arrested over alleged damage to US military plane at Shannon Airport
events.description: A man has been arrested over alleged criminal damage of a US military plane at an Irish airport.

events.title: Chagos Islands deal pulled by government after Trump brands it 'act of great stupidity'
events.description: The government's Chagos Island deal has been pulled after US President Donald Trump branded it an "act of great stupidity".

events.title: Djibouti President Ismaïl Omar Guelleh is reelected for a sixth term
events.description: Djibouti President Ismaïl Omar Guelleh has been reelected for a sixth term, winning 97.81% of the vote in Friday's election

events.title: China says it will resume some ties with Taiwan including more direct flights
events.description: China has announced it will resume some ties with Taiwan including direct flights and imports of aquaculture products

events.title: Benin votes for new president with finance minister favored to succeed Talon
events.description: Voters in Benin are choosing a successor to President Patrice Talon, who is stepping down after a decade in power

events.title: Female rickshaw drivers in Sierra Leone rise above stigma to earn a living and empower women
events.description: A small but growing number of women in Sierra Leone are entering a male-dominated commercial transport sector

events.title: Djibouti holds presidential election with longtime ruler favored for a sixth term
events.description: Voters in Djibouti are casting ballots to elect their president on Friday

events.title: New era for space dawns as Artemis astronauts return
events.description: After a 10-day journey around the far side of the Moon, the four astronauts on NASA’s Artemis II mission are set to splash down in the Pacific Ocean late on Friday.

events.title: Artemis II crew reflects emotionally on lunar mission after safe return: 'Bonded forever'
events.description: The four Artemis II crew members spoke for the first time on Saturday since returning to Earth on Friday after 10 historic days in space.

events.title: Artemis II crew closes in on Earth as mission ends with Pacific splashdown and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: American couple's Bahamas dinghy was ill-equipped for conditions night of wife's disappearance: friend
events.description: A friend says Brian and Lynette Hooker's 8-foot dinghy was undersized and underpowered for the rough conditions the night she vanished in the Bahamas.

events.title: Artemis II nears end of historic mission with splashdown off California coast
events.description: Artemis II is set to splash down off San Diego as Navy crews prepare to recover the Orion capsule and the four astronauts inside, drawing crowds along the coast.

events.title: Malnourished boy found locked in father's van for more than a year unable to walk, hadn't showered since 2024
events.description: A malnourished 9-year-old boy allegedly locked in his father's van in eastern France was found in the fetal position surrounded by trash and excrement.

events.title: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi in Beijing as Taiwan defense fight intensifies
events.description: Xi Jinping met KMT leader Cheng Li-wun in Beijing as Taiwan's opposition blocks a $40 billion defense budget amid U.S. pressure to approve it.

events.title: Israeli man built bomb lab for Iranian plot targeting ex-PM Bennett, authorities say
events.description: A Haifa man was allegedly recruited by an Iranian handler to build bombs for an assassination plot believed to target ex-PM Naftali Bennett.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israeli man built bomb lab for Iranian plot targeting ex-PM Bennett, authorities say

events.title: Iran's UN ambassador takes swipe at Trump in final hours before Strait of Hormuz deadline
events.description: Iran's UN ambassador is calling one of President Donald Trump's posts on Truth Social on Tuesday “deeply irresponsible" and “profoundly alarming."
